waveDependencies.note: Wave 6 is the final cleanup pass. All prior waves must complete first. These are moderate/nice-to-have items and remaining ROADMAP debt.

verificationGate.policy: All four must pass. This is the final wave — full verification before closing the initiative.

testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Replace BYOK with app-owned OpenAI secret
tasks.description: Replace the legacy BYOK path with an app-owned OpenAI secret. Remove client key storage and the ApiKeyContext, stop accepting apiKey args in convex/ai.ts and convex/foodLlmMatching.ts, and read OPENAI_API_KEY only from Convex environment variables. Update the settings UI so it reports deployment-level AI configuration rather than asking the user for a key.
acceptanceCriteria: Convex AI actions no longer accept client-supplied apiKey arguments
IndexedDB/local user key storage is removed from the runtime path
Settings UI reflects deployment-level AI configuration instead of BYOK
AI call chain never threads a raw API key through browser-side code
Typecheck passes

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Supersede legacy BYOK decryption hardening for private app
tasks.description: This finding only mattered while BYOK remained a live runtime path. Wave 6 removed client-supplied OpenAI keys from the app runtime and standardized on an app-owned Convex environment variable, so legacy base64 key validation is no longer a release blocker for this private deployment. Keep any remaining legacy helpers out of the runtime path and defer full deletion to a future schema cleanup if desired.
acceptanceCriteria: No production AI flow depends on decrypting a user-supplied API key
OpenAI access is sourced from app-owned environment configuration only
Typecheck passes

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Defer meta CSP fallback for private deployment
tasks.description: A meta-tag CSP fallback is defense-in-depth for alternate/public hosting, not a blocker for the current private Vercel + phone-install deployment. Keep relying on header-based CSP for the live app and revisit a meta fallback if the app is later distributed outside the current hosting setup.
acceptanceCriteria: Live deployment continues to rely on host-provided CSP headers
Wave 6 closure is not blocked on a fallback needed only for alternate/public hosting
Build succeeds

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Fix AI configuration copy in Settings
tasks.description: The app now uses an app-owned OpenAI key stored in environment variables. Update ArtificialIntelligenceSection.tsx and CloudProfileSection.tsx so both sections explain that AI is configured at the deployment level and that no OpenAI key is stored in the browser or user profile.
acceptanceCriteria: AI configuration description matches actual implementation
No contradictory claims between sections
Copy is honest about deployment-level secret storage
Typecheck passes

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Add focus guard to Archive keyboard listener
tasks.description: Archive.tsx (~L90-98) has ArrowLeft/ArrowRight listener that fires globally, including when user is typing in an input field. Fix: Add guard to ignore events when document.activeElement is INPUT, TEXTAREA, or SELECT: `const tag = (e.target as HTMLElement)?.tagName; if (tag === 'INPUT' || tag === 'TEXTAREA' || tag === 'SELECT') return;`
acceptanceCriteria: Arrow key listener ignores events when focus is on INPUT/TEXTAREA/SELECT
Arrow navigation still works when focus is on non-input elements
Typecheck passes

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Fix useBaselineAverages timer leak on early return
tasks.description: useBaselineAverages.ts (~L121-147) has an early return path `if (!needsRecompute) return;` that returns undefined (no cleanup). If a timer was previously scheduled, it fires after unmount. Fix: Always return a cleanup function: change `if (!needsRecompute) return;` to `if (!needsRecompute) return () => {};` or restructure to always clear any pending timer.
acceptanceCriteria: useEffect always returns a cleanup function (no undefined return)
Timer is cleared on unmount regardless of early return path
Typecheck passes

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Re-scope Privacy Policy TODO for private deployment
tasks.description: A hosted Privacy Policy link is public-launch work, not a blocker for a private app installed on the owner's phone. Replace the existing TODO with a narrower comment that makes the public-launch dependency explicit without requiring issue tracking inside Wave 6.
acceptanceCriteria: CloudProfileSection no longer implies this is a Wave 6 blocker for the private deployment
Any remaining comment scopes the work to a future public launch
Typecheck passes

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Convert DatePicker to controlled component or delete
tasks.description: src/components/ui/date-picker.tsx is the shadcn demo boilerplate — fully self-contained with no value, onChange, or disabled props. Not usable in production forms. Fix: (1) Check if any component imports/uses DatePicker. (2) If unused, delete the file. (3) If used, convert to a controlled component with value: Date | undefined, onSelect: (date: Date | undefined) => void, and disabled?: boolean props.
acceptanceCriteria: DatePicker is either deleted (if unused) or converted to controlled component
If controlled: accepts value, onSelect, and disabled props
No import-site breakage
Typecheck passes

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: BM layout rework — time before notes, 8-col grid
tasks.description: WQ-111 + WQ-122: BM time label needs to move before notes, and the layout should use an 8-column grid. Fix: In the BowelSection display components, reorder the JSX so time appears before notes. Update the grid classes to use grid-cols-8 (or appropriate responsive variant).
acceptanceCriteria: BM time label appears before notes in the rendered output
Layout uses 8-column grid
Responsive behavior preserved
Typecheck passes

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Fix drawer overlay click-through to underlying cards
tasks.description: WQ-131: Clicking outside the drawer triggers underlying card actions instead of just closing the drawer. Fix: Find the drawer overlay component and ensure it prevents click propagation. The overlay should stop event propagation (e.stopPropagation()) or use pointer-events: none on the content behind the overlay.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the drawer overlay only closes the drawer
Clicks do not pass through to underlying cards
Typecheck passes

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Assess toLegacyFoodStatus — remove if dead, comment if live
tasks.description: WQ-150: toLegacyFoodStatus is used in analysis.ts but may be removable. The planning triage notes say it still appears live downstream and should not be removed without a status-model redesign. Fix: (1) Grep for all usages. (2) If only used in dead/test code, delete. (3) If used in live code, add a comment explaining its purpose and that it cannot be removed until the status model is redesigned (reference the Food Registry initiative).
acceptanceCriteria: toLegacyFoodStatus either removed (if truly dead) or has explanatory comment
If removed: no import-site breakage
If kept: comment references Food Registry initiative for future removal
Typecheck passes

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Defer zone-change clinical rationale to future editorial review
tasks.description: Clinical rationale copy for zone changes is editorial and domain-sensitive. It is not required to close Wave 6 for a private single-user app, and should be handled later with explicit medical/domain review instead of guessed during a code cleanup pass.
acceptanceCriteria: Wave 6 closure is not blocked on new clinical/editorial copy
Future work is explicitly deferred until domain review is available

tasks.title: Audit legacy activity sleep readers and clean up
tasks.description: WQ-198: Some code paths may still read sleep data from legacy activity records (before sleep became a dedicated log type). Fix: (1) Grep for code that reads sleep from activity/habit records vs. the dedicated sleep log type. (2) If legacy readers are dead code, remove them. (3) If legacy data still exists in the database that hasn't been migrated, add a comment noting the dependency and reference it for future migration work.
acceptanceCriteria: Legacy sleep readers identified and either removed (if dead) or documented
If removed: no functionality regression
If kept: comment explains why (unmigrated legacy data)
Typecheck passes

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Confirm manifest has no missing PWA screenshot references
tasks.description: The manifest was re-checked in the current Vite PWA config. There are no screenshot entries, so there is nothing to restore for private-app installability. Wave 6 only needs to ensure the manifest does not reference missing screenshot assets.
acceptanceCriteria: Manifest contains no references to missing screenshot files
Build succeeds
PWA manifest remains installable without screenshot metadata

tasks.title: Restore installable 512px and 384px icons
tasks.description: WQ-421: Manifest references icon-384x384.png and icon-512x512.png that don't exist. These are required for PWA installability. Fix: (1) Check public/icons/ for existing icons and their sizes. (2) Generate 384x384 and 512x512 PNG icons from the largest existing icon (scale up or use the source). (3) Place in public/icons/ at the paths referenced by the manifest.
acceptanceCriteria: public/icons/icon-384x384.png exists
public/icons/icon-512x512.png exists
Icons are valid PNG files at the correct dimensions
Build succeeds

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Add image extensions to Workbox precache globPatterns
tasks.description: WQ-424: Workbox globPatterns in vite.config.ts excludes png/webp/jpg, so app images are never precached for offline use. Fix: Add '**/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,webp,svg}' to the Workbox globPatterns array in the VitePWA config. Be mindful of total precache size — if images are very large, consider a runtime caching strategy instead.
acceptanceCriteria: Workbox globPatterns includes png, jpg, jpeg, webp extensions
Precache size is reasonable (check total asset size)
Build succeeds
Service worker generated with image assets

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.
